text.fullText:
The Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) must implement a Broker Transparency Policy to ensure fairness, accountability, and integrity in the freight brokerage industry. Currently, carriers face significant challenges due to opaque broker practices, including undisclosed rate margins, inaccurate carrier flagging, and unregulated digital monitoring that can unfairly impact business operations.

A clear and enforceable transparency policy should include:
•Mandatory disclosure of rate breakdowns between shippers and carriers.
•Standardized and verifiable criteria for carrier compliance and digital monitoring.
•A formal dispute resolution process for carriers flagged by third-party data sources.
•Limitations on IP tracking and digital surveillance without explicit carrier consent.

Without these protections, carriers remain vulnerable to unjustified rejections, financial losses, and reputational damage due to inaccurate or undisclosed broker assessments. FMCSA must act now to establish transparency standards that promote fair competition and protect the livelihoods of trucking businesses.
